div1.entryFree:
Pain, vb. 1) to put to bodily distress or torment: that kills and --s not, Ant. V, 2, 244. Partic. --ed: give physic to the sick, ease to the --ed, Lucr. 901. to enforce the --ed impotent to smile, LLL V, 2, 864. Superl. --edst: the --edst fiend of hell, Per. IV, 6, 173.
2) to put to labour and trouble: that I have employed and --ed your unknown sovereignty, Meas. V, 391.
